ListItem.Para: Bulk cargo heat supply valve is deactivated closed.
Bulk cargo heat supply fan is deactivated.
Bulk cargo electric heater(s) is deactivated.

MaintenanceProcedure.Para: Deactivate bulk cargo heat system (AMM DDG 21-44-01).

ListItem.Para: Set (P5) BULK CARGO TEMP switch OFF.
Deactivate the bulk cargo heat supply valve closed.
Using a flight deck multi-function display only, open the following circuit breakers and lock with an INOP tag:
SOV CLOSE-BULK CARGO SUPPLY
SOV OPEN-BULK CARGO SUPPLY
Gain access to supply valve.
Turn the open/close knob until valve is in the CLOSED position.
Deactivate the bulk cargo heat supply fan.
Using a flight deck multi-function display only, open the BULK CARGO SUPPLY FAN circuit breaker and lock with an INOP tag.
Gain access to bulk cargo heat supply fan.
Disconnect, cap and stow the electrical connector to the bulk cargo heat supply fan.
Deactivate the bulk cargo electric heater(s).

Effectivities.Effectivity: CN-AA29229 Part C or D incorporated ((VN-A861, VN-A862, VN-A863, VN-A864, VN-A865, VN-A866, VN-A867, VN-A868, VN-A869, VN-A870, VN-A871, VN-A872, VN-A873, VN-A874, VN-A877, VN-A878, VN-A879)

ListItem.Para: Using a flight deck multi-function display only, open the following circuit breakers and lock with an INOP tag.
HEATER-BULK CARGO
HEATER-BULK CARGO 2
Gain access to bulk cargo heat electric heaters.
Disconnect, cap and stow electrical connectors to the bulk cargo heat electric heaters.

OperationProcedure.Para: Set (P5) BULK CARGO TEMP switch OFF.

Note.Para: The CARGO HEAT BULK advisory message will be displayed.
Bulk cargo compartment heat is not available. Temperature sensitive cargo should not be carried in the bulk cargo compartment. Refer the flight NOTOC for details.

MaintenanceProcedure.Para: Deactivate inoperative bulk cargo heater (AMM DDG 21-44-01).

ListItem.Para: For CARGO BULK HEATER 1 inoperative, using a flight deck multi-function display only, open HEATER-BULK CARGO circuit breaker and lock with an INOP tag.
For CARGO BULK HEATER 2 inoperative, using a flight deck multi-function display only, open HEATER-BULK CARGO 2 circuit breaker and lock with an INOP tag.
Gain access to the inoperative bulk cargo heat electric heater.
Disconnect, cap and stow electrical connector to the inoperative bulk cargo heat electric heater.

OperationNote.Para: The bulk cargo heat will operate, but at a reduced heating capability.
The CARGO TEMP BULK OFF light will illuminate and CARGO BULK HEATER 1 or CARGO BULK HEATER 2, as appropriate, will display after landing.

MELSubItem.Title: Bulk CARGO TEMP Switch OFF Light (*)
Bulk CARGO TEMP Switch AUTO Light (*)
